item.title: A Fine Country for Old Men
item.description: Geriatric Americans are hoarding wealth and power.
item.dc:creator: Idrees Kahloon

item.title: How to Tax Billionaires
item.description: Step one: Abolish the estate tax. No, really.
item.dc:creator: Ray D. Madoff

item.title: In Praise of ‘Difficult’ Kids
item.description: Feisty children can be exhausting. They also possess a moral fire that deserves cultivating.
item.dc:creator: Russell Shaw

item.title: Harmeet Dhillon Is Not Wasting Any Time
item.description: She arrived at the Department of Justice with radical changes in mind. One year later, she has completely reshaped the Civil Rights Division.
item.dc:creator: Quinta Jurecic

item.title: Why Can’tEuphoriaGrow Up?
item.description: On the teen drama’s third season, adulthood brings only more misery.
item.dc:creator: Shirley Li

item.title: Illiberalism Is Not Inevitable
item.description: If Viktor Orbán can lose, then his Russian and American admirers can lose too.
item.dc:creator: Anne Applebaum

item.title: Hungary Just Ousted the Unoustable
item.description: Viktor Orbán had support from Moscow and Washington, but not from his own people.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: The Profound Comedy of Outer Space
item.description: A goofy sketch captured the grandeur of the Artemis II mission, but also something bittersweet.
item.dc:creator: Michael Tedder

item.title: Byzantine Room
item.dc:creator: Patricia Lockwood

item.title: Seven Documentaries for Fans of Fiction
item.description: Anybody who enjoys an engrossing story will appreciate these movies about real life.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Bai

item.title: The Looming College-Enrollment Death Spiral
item.description: After many decades of democratization, higher education could once again become a luxury good.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Selingo

item.title: The Conciliator
item.description: Why did Gretchen Whitmer go soft on Trump?
item.dc:creator: Elaine Godfrey

item.title: The Thrill of Childhood Rituals
item.description: The joy of playing tag and creating secret languages has persisted across time and place.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Fattal

item.title: The Iran War Is Putting Pressure on Europe
item.description: Panelists joined to discuss the effects of the conflict on America’s allies, and more.
item.dc:creator: The Editors

item.title: The Romance of the Gas-Station Sign
item.description: A few glowing roadside numerals set the national mood.
item.dc:creator: Ian Bogost

item.title: America Has a New GLP-1 Playbook
item.description: First a shot to lose weight, then a pill to keep it off.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Florko

item.title: Iran Could Speedrun the Vietnamese Path
item.dc:creator: Graeme Wood

item.title: Iran Has the Upper Hand in the Upcoming Negotiations
item.description: Turning the cease-fire into a longer stalemate is a matter of political will.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: Why Trump Chose Vance to Negotiate With Iran
item.description: There is no overlap between what America is demanding and what Iran is offering.
item.dc:creator: Karim Sadjadpour

item.title: Steven Soderbergh’s Strange, Surprising Heist Movie
item.description: The Christophersstarts as the tale of a forgery, but ends as an intimate meditation on art.
item.dc:creator: David Sims
